titleStmt.title: Letter from Vincent G. Forth to Prof. John Earls, 5 March 1919
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Vincent G. Forth

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Forth expresses gratitude on the part of his family for a motion passed by the staff in memory of Francis C. Forth.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ravenswood, Knockbreda Park, Belfast. 5th March 1919. Dear Professor Earls, On behalf of my mother I wish to
thank you for your letter of 3rd inst.
enclosing a copy of the Resolution
passed at the Meeting of the Permanent
Staff and the Occasional Teachers of
the Municipal Technical Institute,
Belfast. My Mother is deeply touched by
the expression of sympathy extended
to her, in her great sorrow and be-
reavement, by the Institute Staff and
will be most grateful if you will be
be good enough to convey to them her
heartfelt thanks; at the same time
expressing my own thanks and those
of my sisters and brother. My Mother takes this opportunity
of expressing her deep appreciation of
the most beautiful floral tribute sent
by the Institute Staff. Yours sincerley, Vincent G. Forth Professor Earls, Municipal Technical Institute, BELFAST.
